behavior_summary: Handles the 'U8P_HIDPAD::gotHit' event for a HIDPAD item. It examines an incoming event parameter and the item's stack quantity and then either initiates a teleport sequence, destroys the item, or does nothing.

When the incoming event parameter equals 1 and the item quantity is 0 or 1: clear/reset a reference (via Kernel::resetRef), spawn a teleport process (teleportToEgg) with arguments that vary by quantity (different small code and destination byte), then suspend the current process (the caller appears to wait for the teleport process).
When the item quantity is 0 or 1 but the incoming event parameter is not 1: the item is destroyed (Item::destroy).
When the item quantity is greater than 1: the handler returns without action.
notable_control_flow: Initial check of the second parameter (BP+0Ah) gates main behavior: only the value '1' leads to teleport logic; other values follow a destroy-or-no-op path.
Quantity branching: Item::getQ() result (BP-04h) splits control into three cases: 0, 1, >1. Only the 0 and 1 branches lead to teleport-or-destroy behavior; >1 exits with no action.
Before spawning teleportToEgg the code calls Kernel::resetRef(..., ProcessType=0x06) — a preparatory/cleanup step that runs only on the teleport paths.

Teleport is started as an independent process (process teleportToEgg) and its return is pushed before the current process calls suspend, implying the current process yields/waits for the teleport flow.
open_questions: Precise semantic meaning of the second parameter (BP+0Ah): it is inferred to be an event/hit flag or a process-type indicator but its canonical name and full semantics are not present.

Exact meaning of the small-word and destination-byte arguments passed to teleportToEgg (e.g., 0x03/0x3E vs 0x01/0x0F): they likely encode teleport subtype/destination but specifics are inferred from constants only.
What exact reference/id is being reset by Kernel::resetRef (the uword arg passed) and why ProcessType 0x06 is used — the reset's broader side effects are inferred but not proven.
Whether 'suspend' always waits for teleportToEgg completion or only yields until another engine condition — behaviour is inferred from call pattern but not explicitly documented.

main_function.name: U8P_HIDPAD::gotHit
main_function.purpose: Respond to a 'U8P_HIDPAD::gotHit' event on a HIDPAD item by checking an event parameter and the item's quantity, then either trigger a teleport flow (for specific event+quantity combinations), destroy the item, or return with no action.
entry_conditions: Invoked on the 'U8P_HIDPAD::gotHit' event (Event 6) for the HIDPAD item.
Receives two parameters: an item identifier/pointer (BP+06h) and an event/hit flag or mode value (BP+0Ah).

function_map.name: U8P_HIDPAD::gotHit
function_map.role: Event handler for HIDPAD 'U8P_HIDPAD::gotHit' (event 6). Orchestrates teleport or destroy behavior based on parameters and item quantity.

function_map.name: Item::getQ
function_map.role: Query the item's quantity/stack count. Used to choose between teleport, destroy, or no-op paths.

function_map.name: Kernel::resetRef
function_map.role: Reset/clear a reference associated with the item/process before initiating teleport. Exact reference semantics are inferred.

function_map.name: U8P_HIDPAD::process teleportToEgg
function_map.role: Spawned process that performs teleportation to an 'egg' destination. Called with (pid, small-code, destination-byte). The caller suspends after starting it.

function_map.name: Item::destroy
function_map.role: Remove/destroy the item when teleport conditions are not met.

external_calls.purpose: Return the item's quantity/stack count to decide branching (0, 1, >1).
Clear or reset a reference related to the item/process prior to teleporting. Likely a cleanup/guard to avoid stale references during teleport.
Spawn a teleport process targeting an 'egg' destination; different small-code and destination-byte values are supplied depending on the item's quantity (separate behavior for qty==0 vs qty==1).
Destroy the item when the event parameter does not match the teleport-triggering value (and quantity is 0 or 1).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Handles the 'U8P_HIDPAD::gotHit' event for a HIDPAD item. It examines an incoming event parameter and the item's stack quantity and then either initiates a teleport sequence, destroys the item, or does nothing.
